title: Delphinium bakerianum
taxonomy: Delphinium bakerianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It belongs to the genus Delphinium and is native to China. The classification of Delphinium bakerianum further breaks down to the kingdom Plantae, and the division Magnoliophyta. The plant's classification is also reflected in its binomial nomenclature, which consists of the genus name Delphinium and the species epithet bakerianum. This classification helps in identifying and distinguishing Delphinium bakerianum from other plant species.
uses: Delphinium bakerianum is a species of plant that has been used in traditional medicine in the Himalayas. Its roots and other parts contain alkaloids, which are believed to have medicinal properties. However, specific information on the medicinal uses of Delphinium bakerianum is scarce and more research is needed to confirm its potential health benefits.
